# Daisy

> Daisy is a local-first meeting recorder and dictation app for macOS (Apple Silicon). It records both sides of any call with no bot, transcribes on-device with Whisper, summarises with the AI provider you choose, and exposes a local MCP server so Claude, Cursor, or any MCP client can read your meetings — all on your own Mac. Open source (Apache 2.0), no account, no subscription, no telemetry.

Daisy is made by Addicted Studio, for people who record client interviews, discovery calls, and internal meetings and don't want transcripts living on a vendor's server. The structural difference vs cloud notetakers (Granola, Otter, Fathom) and dictation apps (Wispr Flow, Wave, Handy, VoiceInk): Daisy keeps recordings on your disk and ships a local MCP server bound to 127.0.0.1, so your own AI agent can query your meetings without anything being uploaded.

Key facts:
- Platform: macOS 14+, Apple Silicon (M1 or newer).
- Recording: microphone + the other side of the call captured via ScreenCaptureKit. No bot joins the meeting; works with Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, Telegram — anything that plays audio on the Mac.
- Transcription: on-device Whisper large-v3 on the Neural Engine. Optional Parakeet engine for dictation.
- Summaries: bring your own AI — Anthropic, OpenAI, Apple Intelligence (on-device, macOS 26), or a local model via Ollama / LM Studio. The key stays in your Keychain; requests go straight from your Mac to the provider, never through Daisy's servers.
- Storage: Markdown files in a folder you choose (e.g. an Obsidian vault). Nothing on a server you don't own.
- MCP server: local, binds only to 127.0.0.1, SSE transport. Nine tools: read tools (list_sessions, get_session, search_sessions, list_folders, list_destinations) let Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, or Continue query your transcripts, summaries, and action items; action tools (resummarize_session, set_session_title, rename_speaker, route_session_to_destination) let the agent regenerate a summary, retitle a session, name a diarized speaker, or push a finished session to Notion / Linear / Slack / a webhook. Action tools are scoped to safe, reversible operations — nothing can delete recordings or edit transcripts.
- Dictation: hold-to-talk, types into any app.
- License: Apache 2.0 (OSI-approved open source). Pricing: free during beta, lifetime after launch — never a subscription.

## Docs
- [Documentation home](https://mydaisy.io/docs): setup, recording modes, and integration guides.
- [Connect Daisy to Claude, Cursor, or any MCP client](https://mydaisy.io/docs/mcp): how an AI agent reads — and acts on — your meeting transcripts over a local 127.0.0.1 MCP server (9 tools: 5 read, 4 act).
- [Installation](https://mydaisy.io/docs/getting-started/installation): download and set up Daisy on Apple Silicon.
- [Your first recording](https://mydaisy.io/docs/getting-started/first-recording): record a meeting end to end.
- [Recording modes](https://mydaisy.io/docs/recording/modes): meeting capture vs dictation.

## Comparisons
- [Daisy vs Granola](https://mydaisy.io/alternatives/granola): the local, open-source alternative to Granola.

## About
- [Privacy](https://mydaisy.io/privacy): what stays local and what (optionally) leaves the machine.
- [Changelog](https://mydaisy.io/changelog): release history.
- [Source code on GitHub (Apache 2.0)](https://github.com/addicted-studio/daisy-app): full source.

## Optional
- [Download for macOS](https://github.com/addicted-studio/daisy-app/releases/latest): latest signed, notarized build.
